description: A planned arrangement of activities across time, including their order, stages, or expected occasions. A schedule records intention and coordination, and it can change. It is not itself a promise, nor does it make anyone available who has not agreed to be.
articulatory_notes: Place the tongue tip lightly at the upper teeth for unvoiced 'th' and let air pass into high-front unrounded 'i'. Bring the lips together for voiced nasal 'm', then round them as the stressed vowel opens into true-mid back 'o'. End with a voiced tap or short trill at the alveolar ridge and central 'a'.
usage_notes: Schedules are countable. Exact calendar and clock information stays in source material, together with any version or identifier that matters. 'winora' names a plan, 'lumethi' a deadline, 'seru' a commitment, and 'thimu' time. A schedule may contain or relate to any of them without proving one.

pillars.solarpunk-values: A shared schedule can coordinate maintenance, care, access, and seasonal work while leaving dependencies and rest in view.
pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: A schedule is not evidence that every person listed agreed, remains available, or can still participate under unchanged conditions.

semantic_domains.activity: an intended arrangement of actions or events
semantic_domains.temporal: planned relations among times and stages
